title: Dalbergia ovalis
intro: Dalbergia ovalis, also known as Philippine rosewood or narra, is a tropical tree species native to the Philippines and parts of Southeast Asia, belonging to the family Fabaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its reported antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Dalbergia ovalis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include decoctions, infusions, and extracts, which are used in traditional medicine to treat a range of health conditions.
taxonomy: Dalbergia ovalis is classified in the following order: Kingdom - Plantae, Clade - Angiosperms, Clade - Eudicots, Clade - Rosids, Order - Fabales, Family - Fabaceae, Genus - Dalbergia, Species - D. ovalis. It belongs to the genus Dalbergia and the family Fabaceae. Dalbergia ovalis is a member of the order Fabales and the clade Rosids. The species is part of the clade Eudicots and the clade Angiosperms. Its classification places it within the larger plant kingdom.
uses: Dalbergia ovalis has been found to have various medicinal uses, with its wood and seeds being traditionally used in folk medicine. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory properties, making it useful for treating ailments such as fever and rheumatism. Additionally, its seeds are believed to have antiseptic properties, which can help to prevent infection and promote wound healing. The plant's wood is also used to create traditional medicine, including treatments for digestive issues and skin conditions. Further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal potential of Dalbergia ovalis.
